mission.title: Install and Configure K3s (Lightweight Kubernetes)
mission.description: K3s is a lightweight Kubernetes distribution designed for resource-constrained environments. It simplifies the installation and management of Kubernetes clusters, making it ideal for edge computing, IoT, and development purposes. Key trade-offs include reduced resource usage and a simplified architecture, but it may lack some advanced features found in full Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install K3s Binary
steps.description:
This step installs the K3s binary on your server, which is the core component needed to run a K3s cluster.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

```

> This command fetches and installs the latest K3s version.

steps.title: Verify K3s Installation
steps.description:
After installation, verify that K3s is running correctly by checking the status of the K3s service.

```bash

systemctl status k3s

```

> This command checks the status of the K3s service.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with your K3s cluster using kubectl.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

```

> This sets the KUBECONFIG environment variable to the K3s kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Nodes in Cluster
steps.description:
Check that the nodes are correctly registered in your K3s cluster.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the K3s cluster.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Optionally, configure additional features like metrics server or ingress controllers.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/k3s-io/k3s/master/manifests/metrics-server.yaml

```

> This installs the metrics server for resource monitoring.

uninstall.title: Uninstall K3s
uninstall.description:
This step removes K3s from your server. Be aware that this will delete all resources in the cluster and may leave orphaned resources.

```bash

/usr/local/bin/k3s-uninstall.sh

```

> This script uninstalls K3s and cleans up associated files.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any orphaned resources that may remain after uninstallation.

```bash

kubectl delete svc --all --namespace kube-system

```

> This command deletes all services in the kube-system namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade K3s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current configuration and resources. This step outlines the upgrade process.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > backup.yaml

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command backs up all resources in the cluster.
> This command fetches and installs the latest K3s version.
> Verify that all nodes are in a Ready state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k3s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The K3s service may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'systemctl start k3s' to start the K3s service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'curl -LO "https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl" && chmod +x ./kubectl && sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeconfig may not be set correctly or K3s is not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure K3s is running and KUBECONFIG is set correctly using 'export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no nodes available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Nodes may not be registered or in a Ready state.

**Fix:**
Check node status with 'kubectl get nodes' and troubleshoot any issues.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for ARM64 architecture, improved metrics server integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of the legacy etcd backend.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure that your configurations are compatible with the new ARM64 support.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with the status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your environment meets the minimum requirements for running K3s.
